REFUND.TITLE: Refund Policy
REFUND.CONTENT:
In cases of force majeure that require trip cancellations, or when the booking system cannot reserve seats, the company will issue a refund within 3 business days after receiving complete documents from the customer.

Documents required for ticket refund are as follows:
  1. Original ticket
  2. Copy of the traveler?s ID card (name must match the ticket)
  3. Copy of the representative?s ID card (if another person processes on behalf of the traveler)
Note:
  For refunds, passengers must contact and collect the refund within 7 business days (government working days) from the date the company notifies them; otherwise, the company reserves the right not to provide the refund.

PRIVACY.TITLE: Privacy Policy
PRIVACY.CONTENT_1: This Personal Data Protection Policy is prepared by NJ Travel and Transport Limited Partnership to explain how the company handles personal data of data subjects, such as collection, storage, use, disclosure, and related rights. This is to ensure that data subjects understand the company?s personal data protection policy. Therefore, the company announces this Personal Data Protection Policy as follows:
PRIVACY.CONTENT_2:
?Personal Data? means information relating to an individual that can identify that person, directly or indirectly, but excludes data of deceased persons in particular. Collection of Personal Data: Personal data will be collected by lawful and fair means. The company will collect and store only data necessary for use, by electronic methods or other methods, in accordance with the stated purposes and as required by law. The company will inform data subjects and obtain consent before or at the time of data collection. Purpose of Personal Data Collection
  1. The company collects, stores, and uses personal data of data subjects for various benefits of data subjects and for the company?s business operations, such as promotional activities, database preparation, and offering benefits based on data subjects? interests, or for any other purpose not prohibited by law, or as required by applicable laws and regulations now and in the future, including consent for the company to transmit, transfer, or disclose data to data processors.
  2. The company will retain personal data of data subjects for the period or as necessary according to the purposes for which consent was given. If the company later changes the purpose, the company will notify data subjects.
      2.1 Restrictions on use and/or disclosure of personal data: The company will use and/or disclose personal data only for the purposes for which consent has been obtained from data subjects, and will supervise employees, officers, or operators not to use and/or disclose personal data beyond the specified purposes or disclose it to third parties, except in the following cases:
        2.1.1 Compliance with the law
        2.1.2 Necessary to prevent or suppress danger to life, body, or health of a person
        2.1.3 Necessary for the legitimate interests of the company or of another person or legal entity
        2.1.4 For the benefit of the data subject, and consent cannot be obtained at that time
        2.1.5 For the benefit of an investigation by an inquiry official or a court proceeding/judgment
        2.1.6 For achieving purposes related to preparation of documents for education, research, or statistics, with appropriate safeguards, information security measures, and data quality controls

Personal data we collect
        1. Customer details: first name, last name, gender, telephone number
        2. Payment details
        3. Details of service points regularly used by customers
        4. Our communications with customers
        5. Platform usage records and customer profile images

BUSINESS.TITLE: Terms and Conditions of Service
BUSINESS.CONTENT:
1. Ticket sales are divided into 2 types:
    ? Regular ticket sales: purchasing tickets within 12 hours before departure time from origin and until the vehicle arrives at that station
    ? Advance ticket sales: purchasing tickets at least 12 hours before departure time from origin, and advance booking is available up to 60 days
  2. The condition for postponing open-date tickets has been canceled.
  3. Ticket changes can only be made as date-specified changes.
  4. Ticket change can be made only once without fee. If a ticket has already been changed, it cannot be changed again and must be used for travel only. Note: Ticket changes can be made through 3 channels:
    1. Sales offices at all 3 stations
    2. https://www.facebook.com/nj.phuyaipu
    3. Date-specified ticket changes can set a travel date within 30 days only, counted from the date of the change request
    4. Ticket changes must be made at least 24 hours before the original departure time
  5. Reprinting tickets is divided into 2 cases:
    ? Reprint with fee: lost, torn, forgotten ticket, or tickets purchased and printed at another station, etc.
    ? Reprint without fee: tickets purchased via internet channel, or printer problems during transaction, power outage, system failure, etc.
  6. Ticket cancellation conditions are allowed in the following cases:
    1. Force majeure causing trip suspension, such as natural disasters or political situations
    2. Passenger emergency, illness, or accident: a medical certificate must be submitted within 3 days from travel date; otherwise, travel rights are forfeited Note: Cases other than those above cannot be canceled.
  7. Refund conditions In cases of force majeure that require trip suspension, or when the booking system cannot reserve seats, the company will refund within 3 business days after receiving complete documents from the customer.
Documents required for ticket refund are as follows:
    1. Original ticket
    2. Copy of the traveler?s ID card (name must match the ticket)
    3. Copy of the representative?s ID card (if another person processes on behalf of the traveler)
    Note:
      1. For refunds, passengers must contact and collect the refund within 7 business days (government working days) from the date the company notifies them; otherwise, the company reserves the right not to provide the refund
      2. If the passenger does not travel on the specified date/time stated in the ticket after accepting these conditions, the travel right is considered forfeited
      3. The company reserves the right to change these terms and conditions for online ticket purchasing service without prior notice
  8. Passenger groups eligible for fare discounts under Department of Land Transport conditions are divided into 3 types:
  ? Children with height not exceeding 130 cm (if not wearing a student uniform)
  ? Monks
  ? Soldiers and police officers in uniform
  9. If a passenger?s discount card is close to expiration and the passenger wants to buy an advance ticket where the travel date matches the card?s expiration date, the passenger can proceed normally based on the purchase date.
  10. If a passenger wants to use fare-discount rights but asks a relative/friend to buy the ticket without presenting the original discount evidence card, the discount cannot be used unless the original evidence is presented.
  11. If a passenger used a fare-discount card but cannot travel and wants to transfer travel to a relative/friend, it can be changed without fee, but fare difference must be paid.

    Temporary ticket conditions due to COVID-19 situation
      Effective from April 30, 2021 onward, or until further notice
    Ticket postponement
      1. Date-specified postponement
        ? Customers can request ticket postponement before vehicle departure from origin within 2 hours
        ? Date-selection period extended to 60 days
        ? If the 60-day period has ended and the situation is still unresolved, passengers can reissue replacement tickets at Greenbus ticket sales points
      2. Open-date postponement
        ? Customers can request ticket postponement before vehicle departure from origin within 24 hours
        ? Ticket validity is 60 days from postponement date; passengers must specify the travel date within 60 days from the postponement date

    Travel conditions for passengers
      For your benefit, please read the following travel conditions:
        1. Passengers must comply with the Land Transport Act B.E. 2522 (1979), ministerial regulations, and other related transport laws
        2. Passengers must comply with the company?s transport conditions and acknowledge/accept these passenger travel announcements as stated herein
        3. Passengers must be ready and arrive at the passenger terminal at least 30 minutes before departure to check tickets
        4. The company will not accept baggage containing flammable objects, pets, illegal items, war weapons, strong-smelling food, objects containing liquid, fragile objects, improperly packed items, or oversized/heavy items unsuitable for transport
        5. The company reserves the right to change unacceptable baggage categories without prior notice; in case of damage, the company is not liable and passengers are responsible for all damages
        6. The carrier is not liable for passengers or baggage due to delays, events beyond control, or inherent deterioration damage, etc.
        7. If a passenger does not travel on the specified date/time in the agreed ticket, the travel right is considered forfeited
